data.title: Asset Manager F/m Seeks SEC Approval to Tokenize Treasury ETF Shares
data.description:
F/m Investments has asked the United States Securities and Exchange Commission to allow it to tokenize shares of its flagship Treasury exchange-traded fund.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/asset-manager-f-m-seeks-sec-approval-to-tokenize-treasury-etf-shares/

data.title: US Dollar Faces Volatility Amid De-Dollarization Debates
data.description: The US dollar is currently experiencing volatility, leading to discussions about a potential 'soft era' and de-dollarization. According to NS3.AI, despite these shifts and challenges, the US dollar continues to be the dominant global reserve currency, with no viable alternative posing a threat to its position. Analysts suggest that while there is a gradual reduction in reliance on the dollar in global trade, a complete displacement of the dollar as the leading currency is improbable in the near future.

data.title: Ethereum's Fusaka Upgrade Sparks Temporary Activity Surge, Says JPMorgan
data.description: Ethereum's recent Fusaka upgrade has resulted in a temporary increase in transaction volume and active addresses, according to NS3.AI. However, JPMorgan anticipates that this surge will be short-lived as users increasingly migrate to Layer 2 solutions and alternative platforms such as Solana. The bank notes a decline in interest in NFTs and memecoins, which could lead to a reduction in Ethereum's network fee burns and a gradual increase in ETH supply.

data.title: Ethereum's Fusaka Upgrade Boosts Network Activity, Faces Long-Term Challenges
data.description:
Ethereum's Fusaka upgrade, completed in December 2025, has significantly increased network activity in the short term, according to PANews. The upgrade enhanced each block's data capacity, effectively reducing transaction fees and boosting transaction numbers and active addresses.

However, JPMorgan expressed skepticism about the sustainability of this activity rebound. The report noted that historically, Ethereum's upgrades have not led to sustained growth in network activity. Currently, Ethereum faces structural pressure from Layer 2 networks like Base and Arbitrum, as well as competing public chains such as Solana. Additionally, the decline in speculative interest related to NFTs and meme coins, along with the migration of major applications like Uniswap to dedicated chains, has resulted in capital outflow. These factors have contributed to reduced fee burning on the Ethereum mainnet, increased ETH supply, and a decrease in total value locked (TVL) measured in ETH.

Despite the short-term boost from the Fusaka upgrade, these ongoing challenges cast a shadow over Ethereum's long-term growth prospects.

data.title: Zelenskyy Announces Progress on Peace Document with Russia
data.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has announced that a formal document intended to end the war with Russia is nearing completion. According to NS3.AI, this development suggests potential advancement in efforts to resolve the ongoing conflict. However, specifics regarding the document's contents or the timeline for its signing have not been disclosed.

data.title: Fed mouthpiece: Trump worries Fed chair nominees will change their stance and raise interest rates after taking office.
data.description: Federal Reserve mouthpiece Nick Timiraos wrote on the X platform that Trump's speech in Davos revealed his views on the selection of a new Fed chair. Trump stated that he will announce the new Fed chair in the near future, claiming that all the candidates he interviewed were excellent, but he worried that they might change their stance and raise interest rates after taking office. Furthermore, Trump expressed dissatisfaction with current Fed Chair Jerome Powell, saying he was slow to react on interest rate adjustments.

data.title: Circle founder: A 40% compound annual growth rate for stablecoins is reasonable.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reports that Circle founder and CEO Jeremy Allaire, in an interview with Squawk Box during Davos, stated that a 40% compound annual growth rate is a fairly reasonable benchmark for stablecoins.

data.title: 21Shares Launches First SEC-Approved Spot Dogecoin ETF on Nasdaq
data.description: On January 22, the first SEC-approved spot Dogecoin ETF, supported by the Dogecoin Foundation, was launched by 21Shares on Nasdaq under the trading code TDOG. According to PANews, this ETF allows both retail and institutional investors to access Dogecoin through traditional brokerage accounts. The president of 21Shares stated that the primary target audience for this ETF is young, affluent mainstream investors. This marks the third spot Dogecoin ETF to enter the U.S. market, following similar products from Bitwise and Grayscale.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Opens with Gains as Alibaba Plans AI Chip IPO
data.description: The U.S. stock market opened with positive momentum, as major indices showed gains. According to ChainCatcher, the Dow Jones Industrial Average increased by 0.5%, the S&P 500 rose by 0.65%, and the Nasdaq Composite climbed by 0.95%. Alibaba's shares surged over 4% following the company's announcement of plans to initiate an IPO for its AI chip manufacturing division, Pingtouge. Meanwhile, Procter & Gamble saw a 1.8% rise in its stock value, whereas Abbott Laboratories experienced a decline of over 10% due to significant reductions in both revenue and net profit.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Opens Strong as Crypto Sector Shows Mixed Performance
data.description: The U.S. stock market opened with significant gains, as the Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq all rose by more than 0.6%. According to NS3.AI, the crypto sector experienced mixed results, with Solana leading the gains at 2.32%, while other tokens such as SharpLink and Bitmine saw slight declines. The report also highlights msx.com as a decentralized platform that lists a variety of Real-World Asset (RWA) tokens, including U.S. stocks and ETF tokens.

data.title: US stocks opened mixed: Crypto stocks were up 0.20%, with Strategy (MSTR) up 0.20%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the opening of the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.64%, the S&P 500 rose 0.64%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.90%. The crypto sector showed mixed performance: Solana rose 2.32%, Circle rose 1.39%, Coinbase rose 1.31%, Strategy (MSTR) rose 0.20%, SharpLink fell 0.80%, and Bitmine fell 0.20%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Hackers Exploit Worm Propagation Techniques to Target Telegram Accounts
data.description: Certain hacker groups are employing worm propagation techniques to compromise Telegram accounts, according to Foresight News. These groups contact individuals in both Chinese and English after gaining access to Telegram accounts, using fake Zoom meeting software, malicious code repositories, and infected third-party tools or gaming software to target contacts. Once they have stolen cryptocurrency and account information, the hackers proceed with further operations, continuously refining their worm propagation strategies.

data.title: Michael Saylor Considers Additional Bitcoin Purchases
data.description: Michael Saylor, founder of Strategy, is contemplating further investments in Bitcoin. According to ChainCatcher, Strategy has already spent approximately $2.13 billion to acquire 22,305 Bitcoins, with an average purchase price of around $95,284 per Bitcoin. Currently, Strategy holds a total of 709,715 Bitcoins, having invested approximately $53.92 billion, with an average holding price of about $75,979 per Bitcoin.

data.title: Michael Saylor: Considering buying more Bitcoin
data.description: Strategy founder Michael Saylor stated that he is considering buying more Bitcoin. Strategy spent approximately $2.13 billion to purchase 22,305 Bitcoins, with an average transaction price of approximately $95,284 per Bitcoin. Strategy currently holds a total of 709,715 Bitcoins, with a cumulative investment of approximately $53.92 billion and an average holding price of approximately $75,979 per Bitcoin.

data.title: New X ‘starter packs’ face backlash over crypto ‘scam’ accounts
data.description:
Head of Product Nikita Bier added, and swiftly removed, a controversial crypto influencer from the platform's new starter pack feature.
source: https://protos.com/new-x-starter-packs-face-backlash-over-crypto-scam-accounts/

data.title: The largest short position in Hyperliquid BTC held $276 million in short positions, with a floating profit of $5.62 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the top short position holder on Hyperliquid (0xd83...8fd7) has increased its BTC and ETH positions by $11.87 million and is currently rolling over its positions, with a margin utilization rate of 106.5%. This address holds a total of $276 million in short positions, covering BTC, ETH, HYPE, PEPE, and XMR. BTC positions account for 54% of the total, with a liquidation price of $92,315.4; ETH positions account for 36%, with a liquidation price of $3,107.88; and the current unrealized profit is $5.62 million.

data.title: SlowMist Cosine: Hacker group uses stolen Telegram accounts to spread worms and deliver poison.
data.description: SlowMist's Cosmos posted on the X platform that some hacker groups use worm propagation engineering to gain access to Telegram accounts. They then contact contacts in both Chinese and English, using fake Zoom meeting software, infected code repositories, infected third-party tools, or games to deliver malware to their targets. After stealing cryptocurrency and accounts, these groups move on to the next phase, continuously refining their worm propagation techniques.

data.title: Gold Emerges as Key Competitor to US Dollar Amid BRICS Strategy
data.description: Morgan Stanley has identified gold as a primary contender against the US dollar, as BRICS nations intensify efforts to lessen their dependence on USD reserves. According to NS3.AI, the bank underscores the substantial rise in gold accumulation by BRICS countries, noting a more than 30% increase over the past five years. This move is part of a broader strategy to diversify away from assets denominated in US dollars. The ongoing trade tensions and evolving global alliances are accelerating the shift towards a multipolar financial system.

data.title: Nansen Unveils AI-Driven On-Chain Trading Platform
data.description: Nansen has introduced a new AI-powered on-chain trading platform designed to enhance analytics and execution for traders. According to NS3.AI, the platform aims to improve the delivery of insights by integrating advanced technology with trading processes. CEO Alex Svanevik highlights the role of AI as a supportive tool that enhances human decision-making rather than replacing it. He notes that while AI can process extensive on-chain data, it lacks the human elements of conviction and accountability. Svanevik emphasizes the importance of employing diverse strategies and ongoing testing in crypto analysis, underscoring that the ultimate responsibility and risk management lie with human traders.

data.title: xStocks Stock Tokens Achieve $3 Billion in On-Chain Transfer Volume
data.description:
The on-chain transfer volume of xStocks stock tokens has exceeded $3 billion, marking a significant milestone in the digital asset market. According to NS3.AI, decentralized exchange (DEX) trading volume for these tokens has surpassed $500 million, highlighting their increasing popularity among investors. The number of holders has grown to over 57,000, reflecting a broadening base of interest and engagement.

Additionally, the cumulative trading volume across centralized exchanges remains substantial, further indicating the expanding adoption and liquidity of xStocks stock tokens. This growth underscores the evolving landscape of digital assets and their integration into mainstream financial systems.
